summary: forge-jsxy 1.0.108: install-time agent script and base64-decoded payloads warrant manual review
details: The package ships a postinstall script (scripts/postinstall-agent.mjs) and a relay/upload subsystem (dist/discordRelayUpload.js, dist/relayServer.js, dist/secretScan/*) that together perform network requests, base64 decoding of embedded blobs, and ping/HTTP activity. A separate scripts/encode-deployment.mjs encodes deployment defaults that are later decoded at runtime from dist/deploymentDefaults.js. Several files reference fetches to huggingface.co (dist/secretScan/agentStartupAudit.js around lines 485-558) and Discord-relay upload paths with POST calls. Without deeper tracing it is not possible to confirm whether the postinstall agent fetches and executes attacker-controlled bytes, whether the base64 blobs decode to code (executed via require/eval) or to configuration data, and whether the relay/upload paths exfiltrate installer secrets or only forward user-supplied content. The combination of a postinstall agent, base64-decoded embedded payloads, and outbound HTTP from secret-scanning code is consistent with both legitimate dev-tool behavior (HF Spaces deployment, secret-scan telemetry to HF) and with abusive patterns. Routing for human review to verify the postinstall agent's behavior, the contents of the encoded deployment defaults, and the destinations of the relay/upload code paths.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
